2603.16581.title: V-DyKnow: A Dynamic Benchmark for Time-Sensitive Knowledge in Vision Language Models
2603.16581.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are trained on data snapshots of documents, including images and texts. Their training data and evaluation benchmarks are typically static, implicitly treating factual knowledge as time-invariant. However, real-world facts are intrinsically time-sensitive and subject to erratic and periodic changes, causing model predictions to become outdated. We present V-DyKnow, a Visual Dynamic Knowledge benchmark for evaluating time-sensitive factual knowledge in VLMs. Using V-DyKnow, we benchmark closed- and open-source VLMs and analyze a) the reliability (correctness and consistency) of model responses across modalities and input perturbations; b) the efficacy of knowledge editing and multi-modal RAG methods for knowledge updates across modalities; and c) the sources of outdated predictions, through data and mechanistic analysis. Our results show that VLMs frequently output outdated facts, reflecting outdated snapshots used in the (pre-)training phase. Factual reliability degrades from textual to visual stimuli, even when entities are correctly recognized. Besides, existing alignment approaches fail to consistently update the models' knowledge across modalities. Together, these findings highlight fundamental limitations in how current VLMs acquire and update time-sensitive knowledge across modalities. We release the benchmark, code, and evaluation data.
2603.16581.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型（VLMs）是基於文件的數據快照進行訓練的，包括圖像和文本。它們的訓練數據和評估基準通常是靜態的，隱含地將事實知識視為時間不變的。然而，現實世界的事實本質上是時間敏感的，並且會受到不穩定和周期性變化的影響，導致模型預測變得過時。我們提出了 V-DyKnow，這是一個視覺動態知識基準，用於評估 VLM 中時間敏感的事實知識。使用 V-DyKnow，我們對封閉源和開放源的 VLM 進行基準測試，並分析 a) 模型在不同模態和輸入擾動下的響應的可靠性（正確性和一致性）；b) 知識編輯和多模態 RAG 方法在不同模態下進行知識更新的有效性；以及 c) 通過數據和機械分析，過時預測的來源。我們的結果顯示，VLM 經常輸出過時的事實，反映出在（預）訓練階段使用的過時快照。即使實體被正確識別，事實的可靠性也會從文本刺激降級到視覺刺激。此外，現有的對齊方法未能在不同模態之間一致地更新模型的知識。這些發現共同突顯了當前 VLM 在獲取和更新跨模態的時間敏感知識方面的基本限制。我們釋放了基準、代碼和評估數據。
2603.16581.author: Seyed Mahed Mousavi et.al.
2603.16581.authors: Seyed Mahed Mousavi, Christian Moiola, Massimo Rizzoli, Simone Alghisi, Giuseppe Riccardi
